description: Relies heavily on Eulerian circuits connecting the grid to trace segmented wing geometries in a single continuous line.
Bridges curved segments combining graph-theoretic cycles that enclose maximum grid points while preserving the motif.
Continuous meandering border lines forming sine-wave-like oscillations acting as endless knots.
Breaks down complex curves into piecewise linear or arc combinations on a modular grid approach.
Traverses optimal paths across the underlying pulli matrix using closely packed rhombuses.
Modeled using polar coordinates to map intersecting petals out from the central origin.
Designed as smaller, discrete sub-graphs where nodes form specific non-crossing pairings to guide paths.
Uses pure straight lines and right-angle connections as complex cellular automata arrays.
Alternating knots forming a single Unknot or link of Unknots crossing following strict over-under rules.
Generates a minimal set of closed, non-intersecting curved loops woven through a hexagonal or orthogonal grid.
Combines the non-symmetric calligraphy at the origin (0,0) with balanced traditional cyclic embellishments.
Recursive geometric additions where each feather acts as a self-similar fractal sub-pattern.
